slides.title: Time and Daily Life
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 3: Time and Daily Life. Welcome to Unit 3! In this unit you will learn all about time and daily life. You will name the twelve months of the year and put them in order. You will talk about your daily routine — what you do at each hour of the day. You will learn to talk about the past (what already happened), the present (what happens every day), and the future (what will happen soon). By the end, you will be able to make your own calendar, just like Sarah does in this unit's story, “The Calendar on the Wall”. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and spell the twelve months of the year, in order
Talk about your daily routine using the hours of the day
Use the Present Simple to talk about what you do every day
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and spell the twelve months of the year, in order. you will also talk about your daily routine using the hours of the day. you will also use the present simple to talk about what you do every day. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: January, February, March, April, May, June. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes My Day, Hour by Hour and The Twelve Months. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Simple
bullets: We use the Present Simple to talk about things that happen again and again, and about facts that are always true. It is the “routine tense”. When we say...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Simple. We use the Present Simple to talk about things that happen again and again, and about facts that are always true. It is the “routine tense”. When we say “She marks the calendar every morning,” we mean she does it regularly. This helps us describe our daily life clearly. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — My Daily Routine. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
